ground_truth_fix:
It is just a minor thing (for me) as I have a workaround, but I wonder if it is intended to work only with u32/i32 for PerfEventArray, if this is a missing feature or some limitation in the Linux Kernel itself?
The eBPF VM doesn't support 128-bit values - u128 - which is likely the issue that you're seeing.
u64 or i64 will work just fine in a PerfEventArray.
In the case of IPv6 though you'll be better served with [u16;8] or [u8;16] though since these can easily be converted to an std::net::Ipv6Addr.
